title: Content Detection and Parsing
description: This cluster encompasses a suite of classes responsible for content detection, parsing, and metadata extraction in the Apache Tika framework. The `Tika` class acts as a facade, integrating various detectors and parsers to identify MIME types and extract content from diverse file formats. The `ConfigDeserializer` and `JsonConfig` classes manage configuration deserialization, ensuring flexibility and extensibility. Classes like `EmbeddedLimits`, `OutputLimits`, and `TimeoutLimits` enforce resource and performance constraints, enhancing security and maintainability. The `Detector` and `EncodingDetector` interfaces, along with their implementations, provide robust mechanisms for content type and encoding detection. The `Parser` interface and its implementations, such as `AutoDetectParser` and `RecursiveParserWrapper`, handle the actual parsing of documents, supporting a wide range of media types. The `ContentHandler` and `Metadata` classes facilitate the processing and storage of parsed content and metadata. This cluster leverages Java, the SAX API, and various utility classes from the Apache Tika and Commons libraries to achieve its goals.

title: Charset Detection and Neural Models
description: This cluster encompasses components for character set detection and neural network-based media type prediction. `CharsetSupersets` maps detected charsets to safer supersets, ensuring broader character support. `EncodingDetectorContext` and its inner `Result` class manage and aggregate encoding detection results from various detectors, enhancing accuracy and reliability. `EncodingResult` and `ResultType` encapsulate and categorize detection outcomes, providing structured output. `NNExampleModelDetector` and `NNTrainedModel` utilize neural networks for media type detection, leveraging pre-trained models for efficient and accurate predictions. `NNTrainedModelBuilder` constructs these models, and `TextStatistics` provides byte frequency analysis for text encoding determination. The cluster emphasizes robustness, accuracy, and extensibility, using Java and Apache Tika's framework.

files: org.apache.tika.detect.DetectHelper; org.apache.tika.exception.WriteLimitReachedException; org.apache.tika.io.BoundedInputStream; org.apache.tika.io.FilenameUtils; org.apache.tika.utils.ExceptionUtils; org.apache.tika.utils.ParserUtils; org.apache.tika.utils.StringUtils
title: Content Detection and Stream Management
description: This cluster comprises utility classes for content detection, stream management, and exception handling in the Apache Tika library. `DetectHelper` and `BoundedInputStream` manage partial file analysis and byte limits, respectively, optimizing memory usage and preventing excessive disk spooling. `WriteLimitReachedException` and `ExceptionUtils` ensure robust error handling and stack trace management, enhancing system reliability. `FilenameUtils` and `StringUtils` provide string and filename sanitization, crucial for data integrity. `ParserUtils` facilitates metadata management and parser error tracking, supporting maintainability and extensibility. These components collectively achieve high performance, security, and maintainability, leveraging Java, Apache Commons, and regular expressions.

title: Temporary Resource and Process Management
description: This cluster comprises classes and interfaces for managing temporary resources and external processes. `TemporaryResources` handles the creation and cleanup of temporary files, ensuring they are deleted when no longer needed. `ExternalProcess` defines metadata properties for capturing the output and status of external processes. `FileProcessResult` encapsulates the results of file processing operations, including standard output, error, and exit values. `ProcessUtils` provides utility methods for creating, managing, and cleaning up external processes, ensuring proper handling of timeouts and output capture. Together, these components ensure robust, secure, and maintainable management of temporary files and external processes, leveraging Java I/O, concurrency, and logging frameworks.

files: org.apache.tika.io.TikaInputStream; org.apache.tika.metadata.Metadata; org.apache.tika.metadata.Property; org.apache.tika.metadata.TikaCoreProperties; org.apache.tika.mime.MediaType; org.apache.tika.mime.MediaTypeRegistry; org.apache.tika.parser.ParseContext
title: Content Parsing and Metadata Management
description: This cluster comprises classes for efficient content parsing and metadata management. `TikaInputStream` handles various input types, providing caching and metadata support. `Metadata` and `Property` classes manage and define metadata attributes, ensuring thread-safe storage and standardized property definitions. `TikaCoreProperties` defines core metadata properties for consistency across file formats. `MediaType` and `MediaTypeRegistry` manage media types and their relationships, ensuring compliance with RFC 2045. `ParseContext` facilitates passing contextual information to parsers, supporting serialization and JSON configurations. Together, these components ensure scalable, secure, and maintainable content processing, leveraging Apache Tika and standard Java collections.
